````markdown
- Computer Use Rust-boundary verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build computer_use` passed 3 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, covering strict action/coordinate/text/direction validation and an honest `ComputerUseBridge` unavailable error instead of a delegate placeholder; `cargo test --lib --features pro-build computer_placeholder_handler_is_not_registered` passed, and the broader `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests` passed 31 tests including the no-registration guard; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/computer_use.rs`, `git diff --check`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- PKM note tools bounds verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build note_tools` passed 6 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including template variable caps, research note validation/count caps, citation input caps, Markdown table escaping/row caps, symlinked directory skipping in note inventory, and registry presence; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/note_tools.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- PKM note-tool argument-honesty verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build note_tools` passed 9 Rust unit tests/source-filter matches in `agent_core`, including strict non-string citation format rejection, strict Markdown table action validation, unknown-action errors, non-object JSON row rejection, malformed/empty CSV delimiter rejection, and the existing registry presence guard; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/note_tools.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Curated memory tool verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::memory` passed 5 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including invalid action/target rejection, first-entry char accounting, empty replace/remove substring rejection, replacement char-limit enforcement, and oversized on-disk memory-file rejection. The broader `cargo test --lib --features pro-build memory` filter is not a valid slice gate here because it also selects unrelated `shared_memory::*` mmap tests that fail in this sandbox with `Operation not permitted`; the curated-memory tests passed in that run. `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/memory.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Curated memory argument-honesty verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::memory` passed 6 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including strict missing `action`, malformed `target`, missing `content`, and malformed `substring` argument errors in addition to the existing curated-memory bounds. The broader `memory` filter remains an invalid slice gate because it selects unrelated sandbox-blocked mmap tests. `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/memory.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Legacy file-ops argument-honesty verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build file_ops` passed 7 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including strict missing/unknown action errors, missing path/content/find validation, malformed replace validation, strict line-range typing/order, and a read-range-past-EOF no-panic check; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/file_ops.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Workspace code-search argument-honesty verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build workspace_search` passed 19 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including strict malformed extension array/item validation, result/context range validation, symbol-handler bounds, schema parsing, and existing symbol/dependency extraction coverage; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/workspace_search.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Cron scheduling CRUD verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build scheduling` passed 8 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including persistent-schedule acknowledgement, prompt caps, create/list, update, pause/resume, remove, and cron parsing; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/scheduling.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Delegate-backed inference verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build inference` passed 22 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including route-objective caps, SSM session/label bounds, constrained prompt/custom-grammar/tool-payload/token bounds, and redacted non-JSON delegate-output failures for `ssm_resume` and `constrained_generate`; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/inference.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- macOS delegate tool verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build macos` passed 13 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including `perceive` app-name bounds, `interact` value/type bounds, `screen_watch` timeout validation, and redacted non-JSON delegate-output failures for `perceive`, `interact`, and `screen_watch`; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/macos.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Intelligence delegate verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build nightbrain_trigger` passed 6 Rust unit tests, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build inline_partner` passed 5 Rust unit tests, and `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::intelligence::tests` passed 24 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including non-string priority rejection, inline note-id/cursor bounds, and redacted non-JSON delegate-output failures for `nightbrain_trigger` and `inline_partner`; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/intelligence.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Clarify delegate verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build clarify` passed 7 Rust unit tests in `agent_core`, including question/choice/delegate-response caps, non-string choice rejection, and redacted non-JSON delegate-output failure; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/clarify.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Delegate task verification: `cargo test --lib --features pro-build delegate_task` passed 6 Rust unit tests/source guards in `agent_core`, including missing/blank/oversized objective rejection, max-depth rejection before spawn, and subagent text truncation behavior; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check agent_core/src/tools/delegate_task.rs`, `git diff --check`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::registry::tier_tests`, and `./Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` passed.
- Manual UI verification blocker: Computer Use `list_apps` timed out after 120 seconds, so the code-editor search, semantic-LSP runtime click/selection smokes, typed `.epdoc` link/wikilink GUI smoke, Image slash-command safe/unsafe insertion smoke, Settings Shared Arena row visibility smoke, and launched-app local-model picker memory-pressure/Time Profiler stress are not claimed as passed in this audit.

Build matrix:
- Actual schemes/configurations — wired · `xcodebuild -list -project Epistemos.xcodeproj` reports Debug/Release configurations and schemes `Epistemos` + `Epistemos-AppStore` for the app. · keep as the release matrix source of truth.
- Main Epistemos build — wired · `Epistemos.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` direct target `Epistemos` links `graph_engine`, `syntax_core`, `omega_mcp`, `omega_ax`, `epistemos_core`, `agent_core`, `epistemos_shadow`, `epistemos_code_index`, and `substrate_rt`; Swift conditions are `DEBUG EPISTEMOS_LINK_SUBSTRATE_RT` in Debug and no MAS flags in Release. · treat as direct/Pro-capable build, not MAS.
- MAS/App Store build — gated-correctly · target `Epistemos-AppStore` uses `Epistemos/Epistemos-AppStore.entitlements`, bundle id `com.epistemos.appstore`, and Swift conditions `EPISTEMOS_APP_STORE MAS_SANDBOX EPISTEMOS_LINK_SUBSTRATE_RT`; it does not link `omega_ax`. · keep subprocess/automation surfaces compiled out or stubbed.
- Pro-only power surfaces — wired but compile-flag-confused · direct build links `omega_ax` and uses permissive direct entitlements (`allow-jit`, unsigned executable memory, library validation disabled, network, user-selected files, Apple Events); Rust has `agent_core` `pro-build` feature gates. No Swift `PRO_BUILD` compile condition is defined in the project. · document direct build as Pro-capable and avoid claiming a separate Swift Pro tier until a real flag exists.
- Research/deferred surfaces — gated/deferred · Rust `agent_core` has opt-in `research`; `epistemos-research` defaults empty and requires `--features research`; HELIOS docs/tests/source scaffolds are present but are not v1 runtime authority. · keep out of MAS/runtime claims.
- Current confusion — corrected for this slice · `HELIOSInvariantSourceGuardTests` historically promoted HELIOS Settings toggles as visible despite the v1 freeze; this pass changes the guard to require no visible v1 runtime toggles and no placeholder VRM chat chip, verified by the focused Xcode test command above. · keep future HELIOS guards source-preservation-only unless WRV gates are satisfied.

- Launch privacy / passive TCC gates — patched · `ActivityTracker`, `NightBrainService`, and `TrainingScheduler` no longer call system-wide `CGEventSource.secondsSinceLastEventType` during passive launch/idle checks; `IMessageNativeSetupDoctor.currentStatus(probeAutomation:)` is passive by default and only probes Automation permission from explicit refresh/guided setup; `NightBrainService.canStart()` now requires search/graph/cloud dependencies before starting maintenance. Verified by the 405-test focused source-guard suite above. · require a fresh launched-app log pass before marking the `kTCCServiceListenEvent` / automation prompt regression Verified Fixed
- Metal drawable lifecycle — patched · `MetalGraphView.pauseEngine()` now uses a 1x1 paused drawable size instead of setting CAMetalLayer to 0x0, and `LandingWaveMetalView.Coordinator.draw(in:)` renders synchronously on the main actor with a per-frame reentrancy guard and positive-size guard instead of queuing a late async render against `currentDrawable`. Verified by the 405-test focused source-guard suite above. · require fresh live logs to confirm the `presented once` and `invalid setDrawableSize width=0 height=0` errors are gone
- Generated build artifact hygiene — patched · `syntax-core/target/aarch64-apple-darwin/debug/libsyntax_core.rlib` was a tracked generated artifact even though `syntax-core/target/` is ignored; it is now removed from the Git index with `git rm --cached` and left as an ignored local build output. · do not commit target-directory binaries again
- Project synced-folder artifact hygiene — patched · `project.yml` and `Epistemos.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` now exclude local `KnowledgeFusion/.DS_Store` and `KnowledgeFusion/Training/.DS_Store` metadata from both direct and App Store synced app roots, while preserving the existing MOHAWK/MoLoRA research-artifact exclusions; `ProjectInclusionTests` guard synced roots, metadata exclusions, and the App Store-only `omega_ax.swift` binding exclusion without a broad app-hosted filesystem walk. · keep local metadata/build outputs out of bundled app roots
- App Review audit script — patched · `Tools/app-review-audit/app-review-audit.sh` now scans for Swift `Process(`, `Process.init(`, and `Pipe()` patterns in addition to older shell forms, reports them as W26 stage-0 MAS review warnings, and still hard-passes only the bundle/runtime-download/HELIOS-toggle gates. Verified by the script output above and `HELIOSInvariantSourceGuardTests`. · refine from stage-0 warning to target/config-aware MAS failure only after the MAS build/source partition is audited
- Cognitive DAG (Phase 8.A-8.G) — wired · `RustCognitiveDagClient` exposes node count, edge count, and Merkle root through `CognitiveDagHealthRow` in Settings diagnostics; authority/promotion remains future work. · ship-in-v1
- Graph inspect mode shell — gated-correctly · The source-preserved `GraphInspectModeView` had an unmounted synthetic inspect renderer with placeholder circle/parallax layers. It now renders `EmptyView()` as a deferred shell while v1 continues to use `MetalGraphView` plus `HologramNodeInspector`; a graph source guard rejects fake inspect-layer rendering and scans production Swift sources to keep `GraphInspectModeView` / `enterInspectMode()` unmounted. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/GraphPhysicsSettingsAuditTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-020604-92377.xcresult`. · keep scaffold source-preserved; do not expose inspect mode until it renders real graph subsets
- Graph cinematic node rendering — patched · The existing quality-level-0 cinematic Metal path now returns hard stepped monochrome pixel-circle nodes from `node_fragment` before the old water/performance shading branch, uses world-space radius scaling/flooring with no cinematic screen-pixel clamp so camera zoom actually scales nodes, disables cinematic glow instances, and leaves quality-level-2 performance shading/straight-edge behavior unchanged. Graph controls now present the two real modes as Pixel and Fast, with no third visual mode or SVG overlay. The default graph label SDF atlas is regenerated from JetBrains Mono at 1024px/48pt with shader height-aware sampling and reduced blur widening so labels read as crisp mono text rather than the previous soft retro face. Verified by `cargo test --manifest-path graph-engine/Cargo.toml lod_profile_is_zoom_stable_in_cinematic_mode --lib` and the focused `GraphPhysicsSettingsAuditTests` runs above. · ship source patch; require live graph wheel/trackpad zoom smoke in dark/light mode before marking visual RC-ready
- Night Brain Swift maintenance pipeline — wired · `NightBrainService.Job` owns the v1 background-maintenance order; store-backed jobs checkpoint through `EventStore`, missing dependency jobs throw typed `JobExecutionError` and do not report completion, and `AppBootstrap` injects `CloudKnowledgeDistillationService.rebuildAllModelVaults()` lazily for the cloud-knowledge job. Swift coverage includes `CognitiveSubstrateTests` and `RuntimeValidationTests` source guards. · ship-in-v1
- NightBrain dependency readiness — patched · `NightBrainService.runPipeline` now preflights the selected job order before opening an `EventStore` run, so missing SearchIndex, AgentGraphMemory, or cloud-knowledge wiring defers quietly instead of creating an interrupted run and logging an expected dependency as a job failure; races after preflight still interrupt the run with an informational deferred log, while real job exceptions remain errors. Verified by the 272-test NightBrain/RuntimeValidation run above. · ship source patch; require launched-app log smoke before marking runtime Verified Fixed
- FSRS decay / review surfacing — wired · `FSRSDecayStore.recordReview` prefers the Rust `fsrs` scheduler through generated UniFFI for D/S/R updates; the Swift path is now documented and logged only as a minimal timestamp/grade/count/R fallback that does not update difficulty/stability, and `FSRSDecayStateTests.fsrsFallbackIsLabeledAsMinimalStateUpdate` source-guards against returning to “placeholder update” wording. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/FSRSDecayStateTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-011605-23402.xcresult`. · ship-in-v1 with honest degraded behavior when Rust FSRS is unavailable
- Resonance Gate Core — gated-correctly · Rust `agent_core/src/bridge.rs` exports `compute_resonance_signature_core` and generated Swift bindings expose `computeResonanceSignatureCore`; `ResonanceService` now labels the FFI path as wired and the Swift path as a mirror fallback only for previews/tests/non-FFI builds, while `rg -n "ResonanceChip\\(|ResonanceLegendView\\(" Epistemos --glob '*.swift' --glob '!Epistemos/Views/Resonance/*.swift'` found no production mounts for the preview chip/legend UI. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/ResonanceServiceTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-012120-59214.xcresult`. · keep the core service and source-preserved preview UI, but do not claim a user-facing resonance surface until real emission/mount policy is wired
- NightBrain Rust live scheduler — deferred · `agent_core/src/nightbrain/live.rs` currently registers canonical `NoOpTask` placeholders only; this pass changed placeholder runs to return `TaskOutcome::skipped(1)` and the FFI status mapper to emit `name:skipped:0` rather than `name:complete:0`. Verified by `cargo test --lib nightbrain`. · keep diagnostic-only until real task bodies replace the placeholders.
- NightBrain trigger tool — gated-correctly · Pro-build registration keeps `nightbrain_trigger` out of Core/MAS tool catalogs; `agent_core/src/tools/intelligence.rs` now exposes only implemented immediate jobs, removes unsupported `vault_integrity_check`, defaults to `immediate`, rejects malformed/non-immediate priority values, and redacts malformed delegate output so host-owned idle scheduling is not misrepresented as an agent action. `Phase7Bridge` records immediate priority explicitly and still emits bounded provenance for unsupported/missing-bootstrap paths. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build nightbrain_trigger` and focused `Phase7BridgeAgentEventTests`. · ship the Pro-only immediate trigger; keep normal/background scheduling in the host NightBrain idle scheduler.
- Graph semantic embeddings — wired · `EmbeddingService.computeAndPush` keeps fallback embedding computation off the MainActor, the O(n²) Rust semantic-neighbor recompute runs detached, `Engine.semantic_neighbors` and `Engine.embedding_store` are both mutex-protected, and recompute now clones a short-held store snapshot before cosine work so later clear/reset/batch mutations cannot race the detached pass. · ship-in-v1
- Typed artifact route host — gated-correctly · `ArtifactRoute` remains a compile-time route-identity spine, but `ArtifactHostView` is now explicitly labeled as source-preserved and not a v1 production navigation surface; all route bodies render `ArtifactRouteDeferredPanel` with v1-safe copy, and `ArtifactRouteTests.artifactHostViewRemainsDeferredUntilResolversAreReal` scans production Swift sources to verify no `ArtifactHostView(` mount exists while destinations are deferred. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/ArtifactRouteTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-012951-13606.xcresult`. · preserve the typed spine, but do not surface the host until real note/document/run/source/code/output resolvers exist
- Per-model memory / model profiles v2 — wired · `SDModelProfile`, `ModelProfileManager`, `ModelVaultsSettingsView`, `ModelVaultsSidebarSection`, and profile/vault selector views expose model-scoped vault identity and compiled knowledge status. · ship-in-v1
- Curated memory tool — wired · Rust `memory` remains an Agent-tier vault-local curated memory tool; `action` is now required, provided `target/content/substring` fields are strictly typed by action, invalid targets/actions fail as arguments, empty replace/remove substrings are rejected, add/replace/remove roll back on persistence failure, replacement respects store char caps, and oversized on-disk memory files are ignored with a warning. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::memory`; unrelated shared-memory mmap tests remain sandbox-blocked. · ship-in-v1
- Skills + procedural memory + self-evolution — wired · Skill Hub can discover/create/install `SKILL.md` files and Rust context loading includes skills; `skill_manage` remote imports now require strictly typed `allow_remote_skill_install=true`, URL/GitHub inputs reject embedded credentials and host spoofs, remote bodies are bounded, quarantined skills are re-scanned inside the managed quarantine root at approval time, and malformed list/view/legacy CRUD/approve/category fields now fail as argument errors instead of being silently defaulted away. The Pro-only `self_evolve` tool remains read-only/advisory, caps trace reads, reports skipped trace diagnostics instead of silently ignoring oversized/malformed traces, and sanitizes advisory skill slugs before suggesting `skill_manage` follow-up. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build skills`. Generated tool runtime registration and autonomous promotion stay deferred. · ship-in-v1 as advisory/quarantined procedural memory only
- Shell-backed custom tools — gated-correctly · `tool_manage` remains Agent-tier modification; custom JSON specs still load as callable runtime tools, but validation now rejects `read_only` risk and any tier below Agent because `command_template` executes through the terminal handler. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build custom_tools` and `cargo test --lib --features pro-build custom_tool_specs_become_runtime_tools`. · ship-in-v1 as Agent/Full shell automation only
- Terminal/process — gated-correctly · Pro-only terminal/process remains out of MAS/Core; child env uses the shared denylist plus secret stripping, foreground/background subprocesses use `kill_on_drop`/process groups as appropriate, rolling buffers bound background output, and `workdir` now rejects empty/relative/missing directories before spawn. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build terminal`. · ship-in-v1 as explicit Agent-tier subprocess
- Cron scheduling — gated-correctly · Pro-build `cronjob` remains hidden from ChatLite/MAS/Core and manages persisted SQLite job rows only; it does not execute scheduled agent loops itself. `create`, `update`, and `resume` now require `allow_persistent_schedule=true`, and job name/prompt/schedule/id/list outputs are bounded. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build scheduling`. · ship-in-v1 as explicit persistent scheduling CRUD only
- Filesystem tools — wired · `read_file`, `write_file`, `patch`, and `search_files` keep pagination, binary detection, protected path/credential filename blocklists, readback verification, and diff previews; existing-target canonical checks now block safe-looking symlinks that resolve to sensitive filenames before read/write/patch. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build filesystem`. · ship-in-v1
- VaultChatMutator git audit commits — gated-correctly · MAS/App Store builds still perform the verified file write but now return an explicit `mas-file-only-*` reference instead of placeholder wording; direct builds use `/usr/bin/git` directly, pass a minimal git environment with `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, suppress global/system config, and commit with `--no-verify` so vault approvals cannot inherit app secrets or run repository hooks. Verified by focused `VaultChatMutatorTests` including a real git commit. · ship git-backed audit commits in direct builds; keep MAS file-only and honest
- Workspace code search — wired · `workspace_search`, `find_symbol`, `get_function_source`, `get_dependencies`, `get_dependents`, and `get_change_impact` remain ChatLite-visible read-only codebase helpers; their public description now says memory-mapped byte scanning rather than fixed sub-millisecond/ARM64-NEON behavior, and workspace/query/symbol/extension/result/context fields are strictly typed and bounded instead of silently dropping bad extension entries or defaulting malformed limits. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build workspace_search`. · ship-in-v1
- Legacy file ops — gated-correctly · `file_ops` remains registered as an Agent-tier modification scaffold for Goose/Hermes parity, but its path guard now blocks protected system write prefixes and canonical symlink targets to sensitive files, action/path/write/patch fields are strictly typed by action, unknown actions fail as real argument errors, and line ranges are validated to avoid out-of-order slice panics. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build file_ops`. · ship-in-v1 as legacy Agent-only file ops
- Apple app automation — gated-correctly · `apple_notes`, `apple_reminders`, `apple_calendar`, and `apple_mail` remain Agent-tier Pro AppleScript tools; the shared runner now bounds stdout/stderr conversion and redacts raw AppleScript stderr into classified permission/app-state errors. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build apple`. Live Automation permission smokes remain manual. · ship-in-v1 as explicit Agent-tier macOS automation
- Mixture of minds cloud ensemble — gated-correctly · `mixture_of_minds` is a Pro-only external-provider fan-out tool, not a local/offline reasoning feature; its schema now requires strictly typed `allow_cloud_external_requests=true`, validates problem/model-list shape and model names before any network request, rejects malformed model arrays instead of silently dropping entries, and redacts provider transport errors instead of echoing request URLs that could contain query-string credentials. Missing API keys return per-model failures without fake answers. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build mom`. · ship-in-v1 only as explicit Pro cloud fan-out
- Media vision/image cloud tools — gated-correctly · `vision_analyze` is Pro-only external vision over Claude/OpenAI and requires `allow_cloud_external_requests=true` before reading local image bytes or dispatching any provider request; local `image_path` now blocks protected credential paths, sensitive filenames, and symlink targets to those files before read/API-key lookup. It rejects ambiguous URL+path sources, reports `cloud_requests_authorized` on success, and redacts provider body/transport details on failure. `image_generate` still requires an explicit `provider`; the local/delegate MLX lane remains honest when unavailable, and the FAL cloud lane now requires both `provider="fal"` and `allow_cloud_external_requests=true`. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build media` and the ChatPro catalog contract. · ship-in-v1 as explicit Pro cloud/delegate media only
- Text-to-speech — gated-correctly · `text_to_speech` remains Pro-only and uses hardened macOS `say`; live playback requires `allow_audio_playback=true`, `output_path` is treated as a file write with absolute/`~/` path validation, protected system/credential/symlink target blocking, and R.5 file-target inference, and `say` failures are redacted/classified. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build text_to_speech` and the ChatPro catalog contract. · ship-in-v1 as explicit Pro local media subprocess
- Send message — gated-correctly · `send_message` remains Agent-tier/destructive and excluded from ChatLite/ChatPro; explicit Slack/Discord/generic `webhook_url` targets require `allow_custom_webhook_url=true`, malformed target/webhook/consent fields fail before dispatch, Signal recipient arrays no longer drop non-string entries, WhatsApp/email/Matrix recipient fields are validated before credential reads, outbound webhook validation redacts rejected URLs, and provider request/HTTP errors no longer echo response bodies or token-bearing URLs. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::communication::tests`. · ship-in-v1 as destructive Agent-only messaging
- Contact routing — gated-correctly · `imessage_contacts` and `channel_contacts` remain ChatPro modification config tools, but saved routes now cap `tool_tier` at `agent`; `full` is rejected and removed from both schemas so auto-reply/contact routes cannot exceed the documented v1 safety model. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build imessage_contacts` and `cargo test --lib --features pro-build channel_contacts`. · ship-in-v1 as capped route configuration
- Web search/extract/fetch/crawl — wired · `web_search`, `web_extract`, `web_fetch`, and `web_crawl` remain v1-visible; the shared URL guard now parses hosts, rejects embedded credentials and local/private IPv4/IPv6/metadata targets, redirects reuse the same guard, extract/crawl/fetch use bounded response reads, provider/fetch request errors are redacted, query/URL/limit/crawl fields are strictly typed and bounded, `web_extract.urls` no longer drops malformed entries, and `web_fetch` truncates text on UTF-8 character boundaries. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build web`, `cargo test --lib --features pro-build web_fetch`, and `cargo test --lib --features pro-build tools::web::tests`. · ship-in-v1
- Vault knowledge tools — wired · `vault_recall`, `contradiction_check`, `session_search`, and `neural_recall` remain ChatLite-visible vault-native tools; malformed tag filters, optional string fields, limits, and temporal windows now fail as argument errors instead of being silently dropped/clamped, and `vault_recall` now reports measured latency rather than promising a fixed sub-5ms bound. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build knowledge`. · ship-in-v1
- Discovery tools — gated-correctly · `model_catalog` remains ChatLite but now defaults to the local MLX catalog, clamps result limits, and requires `allow_cloud_external_requests=true` before querying OpenRouter through the bounded/redirect-guarded HTTP path; `mcp_discover` remains ChatPro, redacts MCP config credentials from env/header/arg shapes, marks entries with `redacted_secrets`, and now requires `allow_create_missing_dirs=true` before creating config directories, with registry risk corrected to `Modification`. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build discovery` and `cargo test --lib --features pro-build chat_pro_adds_vision_and_tts_over_chat_lite`. · ship-in-v1 as honest local-first discovery
- CLI passthrough (Claude / Codex / Gemini / Kimi) — gated-correctly · Pro-build handlers exist for all four CLIs, use hardened subprocess spawning, require resolved binaries to be executable files, validate optional working directories as absolute existing directories, and return install hints when missing; live smoke passed for installed Claude/Codex/Kimi, while Gemini is absent. · ship-in-v1
- Tirith security scanner — gated-correctly · `tirith` remains Pro-only and optional; it now resolves only installed or already-cached executable binaries and no longer downloads executable code at runtime or claims unimplemented SHA/cosign verification. If unavailable, the existing fail-open/fail-closed policy returns the configured fallback result. · ship only as installed/cached optional scanner
- Computer Use / Visual Verify — gated-correctly · `DeviceAgentService`, `ScreenCaptureService`, and `Screen2AXFusion` are lazy, `VisualVerifyLoop` is intentionally not injected into `AppBootstrap`, and the orphaned Rust `computer` handler is source-guarded out of the registry; if invoked directly it validates action payloads and fails honestly instead of returning a fake Swift-delegate placeholder. Source-guard tests assert the loop stays unreachable for v1, and Rust tests assert the placeholder handler is not registered. Live Accessibility/Screen Recording/UI permission smokes remain manual. · defer-to-v2 except the native Swift `ComputerUseBridge` path already covered by host source guards
- Visual Intelligence App Intent bridge — gated-correctly · `VisualIntelligenceIntents.swift` is macOS-deferred because Apple exposes `semanticContentSearch` as iOS-only here; the macOS facade now logs an explicit unavailable/deferred message and returns no results, stale stub wording is removed, and the iOS `@AppIntent(schema: .visualIntelligence.semanticContentSearch)` bridge is behind `EPISTEMOS_ENABLE_VISUAL_INTELLIGENCE_INTENT` so it cannot quietly ship before pixel-buffer conversion and image-note retrieval are real. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/IndexedEntityTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-021130-17495.xcresult`. · keep source-preserved; do not expose visual search on macOS v1
- CoreML device-action backend — deferred · `AppBootstrap` still probes `CoreMLActionBackendLoader.loadIfAvailable()`, but the loader now hard-gates on `actionModelFeatureMappingEnabled = false`; a compiled `.mlmodelc`/`.mlpackage` in Application Support can no longer preempt AppleOnDevice/SharedGPU with fake `coreml-action-backend-stub` JSON. Direct `CoreMLActionBackend.generate` now throws `DeviceAgentError.backendUnavailable` until feature mapping exists. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/DeviceAgentServiceTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-010956-99575.xcresult`. · keep the slot source-preserved, but do not ship a CoreML action model path until input/output feature mapping, validation, and rollback are implemented
- Omega retired orchestrator — gated-correctly · The live agent path remains main chat -> Rust `agent_core`; the compatibility `OrchestratorState` now fails closed on direct `submitTask`, sets an explicit retired planning error, records a failed execution-log entry, and no longer labels compatibility state as stubs. `OmegaPanel` is still reachable only as a retired/unified-chat message via the Omega utility panel. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/OmegaRetiredOrchestratorTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-013544-41907.xcresult`. · keep compatibility injection until all environment users are migrated, but do not route v1 tasks through Omega
- Omega retired companion views — gated-correctly · `ExecutionProgressView`, `ConfirmationSheet`, `ResearchRequestView`, and `TaskInputBar` are source-preserved compatibility views only; stale stub labels were removed, `TaskInputBar` no longer renders a dead text field/button, and a source guard scans production Swift sources to keep these views unmounted while Omega remains retired. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/OmegaRetiredOrchestratorTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-014231-68783.xcresult`. · keep source-preserved until migration finishes; do not expose dead Omega task UI in v1
- macOS delegate tools — gated-correctly · Pro-build `perceive`, `interact`, and `screen_watch` remain Agent-session delegate tools that require a live Swift host and macOS permissions. The Rust boundary now validates bounded app/target/value/condition/timeout inputs, rejects malformed delegate responses without echoing raw host output, and no longer promises host-owned percept latency. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build macos`; live Accessibility/Screen Recording permission smokes remain manual. · ship-in-v1 as explicit Agent-session delegate tools
- Browser automation — gated-correctly · Pro-build `browser_*` tools wrap the optional `agent-browser` CLI with missing-binary errors; `agent-browser` is not installed here and no Claude-in-Chrome MCP surface was found. `browser_vision` now requires strictly typed `allow_cloud_external_requests=true` before any screenshot is captured for external vision analysis, optional browser flags/expressions are strictly typed before CLI execution, and the CLI wrapper bounds stdout/stderr reads while redacting non-JSON/failure output. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build browser`. Live browser permission smoke remains manual/deferred. · defer-to-v2
- Local agent (LocalAgentLoop / LocalAgentPromptBuilder / ConfidenceRouter) — wired · `LocalAgentLoop` owns MLX/constrained grammar/tool parsing paths, `LocalAgentGatewayPolicy` separates local deterministic answers from external-context routes with tests, and reflex mode now preserves the one-shot fallback when streaming is absent while throwing a typed error if the private reflex path is ever miscalled. · ship-in-v1
- Knowledge Fusion adapter training — gated-correctly · Non-MAS train-on-vault UI remains reachable, but newly trained adapters now register inactive because v1 has no wired quality/deploy evaluator; completion copy says `adapter registered, activate after review`, `activeAdapter` refreshes only from the registry's active set, and a source guard rejects reintroducing immediate `setActive(record.id, active: true)` after registration. Verified by the focused `KnowledgeFusionViewModelTests` run above. · ship manual adapter registration/activation only; do not auto-deploy unscored adapters in v1
- Knowledge Fusion Python environment setup — gated-correctly · `PythonEnvironmentManager` now uses an existing local Python 3.10+ toolchain from deterministic system/Homebrew/python.org candidates, refuses in-app Homebrew/curl/Python bootstrap installs, creates the venv under a bounded noninteractive subprocess environment, and disables inherited user-site/pip prompt/version-check behavior. Verified by the focused `RuntimeValidationTests` + `AppStoreHardeningTests` run above. · ship direct/Pro local-venv setup only with user-installed Python; keep MAS unavailable and do not download executable toolchains from inside the app
- Knowledge Fusion Python subprocess fallbacks — gated-correctly · QLoRA, KTO, AudioTranscriber, and MoLoRA subprocess fallbacks now require executable local tool/scripts, run under the shared bounded Knowledge Fusion environment, capture stdout/stderr through capped async readers, terminate on timeout/cancellation, and surface sanitized bounded diagnostics instead of inheriting the app env or echoing unbounded process output. Verified by the 278-test focused Runtime/App Store hardening suite above. · ship direct/Pro fallback only; keep MAS unavailable and do not present Python fallback as installed unless the local executable/script checks pass
- MoLoRA adapter routing — gated-correctly · `AdapterRouter.routeToken` remains a source-preserved nil-returning Swift per-token scaffold, now explicitly labeled unavailable in v1; `MoLoRARouter` and the optional non-MAS Python subprocess are labeled as prompt-level decide-once routing from layer-0 hidden states, not per-token routing, and `MoLoRAInferenceService.start` no longer has an unconditional `|| true` state guard. Source guard rejects stale Python-side "handled" wording and stale per-token runtime claims. Verified by the focused `AdapterRouterTests` run above. · ship explicit/automatic request-level routing plus optional Pro decide-once MoLoRA only; keep per-token routing deferred until a real MLX/Metal kernel path exists
- Adapter bundle export/import — wired · `AdapterExporter` remains a non-MAS Pro/direct adapter-share path with `ditto` body gates; the round-trip test now writes metadata matching the requested adapter type/rank, asserts exact imported metadata fields, rejects unconditional metadata assertions, and reads adapter source from the real source mirror for the no-fusion safety guard. Verified by the focused `AdapterExporterTests` + `AdapterFusionSafetyTests` run above. · ship-in-v1 for explicit adapter bundle export/import only; do not allow tests to hide metadata loss or model-fusion regressions
- Delegate-backed inference tools — gated-correctly · `route_private` remains the ChatPro read-only audit surface with bounded objectives. `ssm_resume` and `constrained_generate` remain Agent-session delegate tools only; the Rust bridge now validates bounded SSM ids/labels, constrained prompts/custom EBNF/tool payloads/token counts, rejects raw non-JSON delegate output instead of echoing it, and no longer promises storage/latency characteristics owned by the host delegate. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build inference`. · ship-in-v1 as honest delegate bridge only
- Inline partner context — gated-correctly · `inline_partner` remains an Agent-session delegate lookup into the Swift note-editor stack; the Rust bridge now bounds note ids, validates cursor offsets, and rejects malformed delegate output without echoing raw host text. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build inline_partner`. · ship-in-v1 as explicit editor-context delegate only
- Clarify tool — gated-correctly · `clarify` remains the delegate-backed ask-the-user tool; question text, choice count/text, and delegate response size are now bounded, malformed choices are rejected instead of silently dropped, and malformed delegate output is redacted. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build clarify`. · ship-in-v1
- Subagent delegation — gated-correctly · Pro-build `delegate_task` is wired only inside an agent session after provider resolution; objectives are trimmed, bounded, and rejected as real argument errors when missing/blank/oversized, max-depth rejection is a real execution failure, and returned subagent response/error text is bounded. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build delegate_task`. · ship-in-v1 as Agent-tier bounded delegation
- MCP bridge — wired · `AppBootstrap` owns `MCPBridge`, which registers the `omega-mcp` builtin tool catalog and records executions through the local dispatcher; stdio MCP subprocesses still clear inherited env, then now reject dynamic-loader/interpreter-option keys from per-server env while allowing explicit server credentials such as provider API keys. · ship-in-v1
- EventDrain / event ring fallback — gated-correctly · `RustEventRingClient` remains the real `substrate-rt` FFI event-ring path behind `EPISTEMOS_LINK_SUBSTRATE_RT`, while `InMemoryEventRingClient` is now explicitly labeled as fallback/test pre-link behavior with queue label `com.epistemos.eventring.in-memory`; source guard rejects stale `stub` wording in this fallback surface. Verified by the focused `EventDrainTests` run above. · keep deterministic fallback/test behavior only; do not present it as the production Rust event ring unless the FFI client is linked
- Halo Shadow index (W8.4 / W8.7) — wired · `AppBootstrap` opens the production `RustShadowFFIClient`, bootstraps vault notes/chats into Shadow, and feeds `ShadowSearchService` into `HaloController`; continuous FSEvents crawling remains deferred. · ship-in-v1
- Shadow fallback provenance — gated-correctly · The app-open path remains persistent `RealBackend`, while the pre-open/test `ShadowState` path is labeled as an in-memory fallback and emits `in-memory-substring` provenance. Swift's deterministic fallback client is now `InMemoryShadowFFIClient` with queue label `com.epistemos.shadow.in-memory`, and `HaloState` docs match the Rust fallback label; source guards reject stale `stub-substring`, `W8.1 stub`, `ShadowState stub`, `STUB_FALLBACK`, `StubShadowFFIClient`, and `com.epistemos.shadow.stub` labels in the Shadow fallback surface. Verified by `cargo test --lib state::tests` in `epistemos-shadow` plus `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/ShadowServicesTests -only-testing:EpistemosTests/ShadowVaultBootstrapperTests`; latest passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-034007-66697.xcresult`. · keep fallback for deterministic pre-open/test use only; do not present it as persistent Shadow search
- Shadow search health diagnostics — patched · `ShadowSearchDiagnostics` records total searches, failures, consecutive failures, last domain, hit count, latency, and closed failure class from `ShadowSearchService.search` / `searchOrThrow` without persisting or surfacing raw backend details; Settings mounts a read-only `ShadowSearchHealthRow` driven by diagnostic change notifications, so hidden backend failures become an honest degraded state instead of an invisible empty-hit result. Verified by the 27-test Shadow/SearchFusion suite above. · ship-in-v1; require live launched-app failure/success smoke before marking runtime Verified Fixed
- AgentGrep / code-index fallback provenance — gated-correctly · `epistemos-code-index` is built by the app build scripts, but `AgentGrepService` has no production mount/call site yet. The Rust fallback indexer and Swift `InMemoryCodeIndexClient` now label fallback hits as `in-memory-substring` instead of stale `stub-substring`, and a source guard rejects stale W9.7 stub/backend labels and `StubCodeIndexClient` naming across `epistemos-code-index` and `AgentGrepService`. Verified by `cargo test --lib state::tests` in `epistemos-code-index` and `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/AgentGrepServiceTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-020024-65873.xcresult`. · keep fallback/test client only; do not present AgentGrep as production-wired until Swift FFI/client and app reachability are real
- RRF Fusion search (Phases 0-7) — wired · `SearchFusionHealthRow` reports the `EPISTEMOS_RRF_FUSION_V1` flag, latency, p95, per-source hits, and errors from `SearchIndexService` diagnostics. · ship-in-v1
- Provenance Console — wired · Settings exposes `ProvenanceConsoleView` over run/mutation/claim/agent/graph projections, and the Rust `epistemos_trace verify-replay` path exists for bundle verification. · ship-in-v1
- Trajectory export — gated-correctly · Pro-build `trajectory_export` remains Agent-tier/MAS-excluded; `output_path` now requires absolute/`~/` file targets, blocks protected system/credential paths and symlink targets to sensitive files, streams JSONL writes, and caps inline results by session count plus 512 KiB byte budget. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build trajectory`. · ship-in-v1 as explicit Agent-tier export only
- PKM note tools — wired · `note_template`, `note_linker`, `research_digest`, `citation_extractor`, and `markdown_table` are registered v1 tools; this pass adds explicit input/output caps, rejects malformed research note arrays, skips symlinked directories during note inventory, reports inventory truncation, escapes Markdown table cell pipes/newlines, rejects malformed citation format/table action/delimiter fields, returns real unknown-action argument errors, and rejects non-object JSON table rows instead of emitting blank rows. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build note_tools`. · ship-in-v1
- Note Editor Tiptap chrome — wired · Epdoc chrome, slash, bubble, KaTeX, block-context, insert-link, gutter, complexity-meter, thought badge, and bundled Tiptap assets are present with focused source guards/smokes; typed Markdown links and wikilinks now convert to safe link marks through input rules, and visible image URL insertion now uses the same safe image-source policy as typed/pasted Markdown. · ship-in-v1
- Epdoc durable graph projection — patched · `EpdocGraphProjector` now emits bounded authored semantic `.contains` label edges from headings, list items, blockquotes, image alt/title, and long paragraph lead sentences when a long document has no wikilinks, while rejecting wikilink markup, empty/oversized labels, and generic placeholder labels such as `Idea` / `Evidence`; `EpdocGraphPersistence` treats generated `.contains` edges as replaceable projection output on re-save. Verified by the 65-test Epdoc graph/document/query/complexity suite above. · ship-in-v1; require live graph-button smoke before marking runtime Verified Fixed
- Epdoc complexity metadata — patched · `EpdocDocument.fileWrapper(ofType:)` recomputes `manifest.metadata["complexity"]` from the canonical `content.pm.json` on save, preserves existing metadata such as theme, and removes stale complexity only when the payload cannot be scored; `setTitle(_:)` preserves metadata. This backs the existing `EpdocQuery` complexity rules with real save-path metadata. Verified by the 65-test Epdoc graph/document/query/complexity suite above. · ship-in-v1
- Prose editor width/scrollbar geometry — patched · `NoteDetailWorkspaceView.noteEditorSurface` no longer constrains `ProseEditorView` to `NoteDualPreviewLayout.editorReadableWidth`, so the scroll view fills the workspace and the vertical scrollbar stays at the editor surface edge; text readability stays owned by TextKit horizontal insets (`ProseTextView2.horizontalInset`) with updated parity expectations for the wider 960pt text feel. Verified by the 172-test note editor/TK2 suite above. · keep v1 source patch; require live Computer Use visual smoke before marking runtime Verified Fixed
- Note ask toolbar reachability — patched · `AssistantToolbarAskBar` now gives the note ask text field an accessibility label/hint and exposes an explicit icon submit `Button(action: onSubmit)` with the same placeholder label, disabled only for empty trimmed text; streaming still exposes a labeled stop button. Source guards in `NoteToolbarGlowTests` cover the reachable button path. · keep visible v1 note-chat entry; require live AX/click smoke before marking runtime Verified Fixed
- BlockMirror background sync coalescing — patched · `BlockMirrorSyncCoordinator` now waits through the existing short coalescing window and checks the generation before creating a background `ModelContext`, preventing superseded bodies from persisting stale blocks during rapid reschedule. `TextKit2ParityTests` now covers the old failure case, where `Old opening` could survive beside the latest blocks. · ship-in-v1
- Notes sidebar vault selector — gated-correctly · `NotesSidebar` only has a confirmed active-vault row today, so it now mounts `VaultSelectorView(selectionEnabled: false)` as read-only status instead of passing a no-op selection closure; `VaultSelectorView` only renders clickable rows when selection is enabled, the row is inactive, and a real `onSelect` handler exists. Verified by `VaultSelectorSourceGuardTests`. · ship current-vault status in v1; do not expose multi-vault switching until a real known-vault list and switch path are mounted
- Memory + energy hardening — wired · `MLXInferenceService` idle unload and pressure paths call `MetalRuntimeManager.deepUnload`, runtime diagnostics log pressure state, WK process-pool reset logic is present, Settings includes read-only Shared Arena diagnostics plus a read-only Process Memory row reporting RSS and memory-pressure state without claiming allocation root cause, and `LocalModelToolbarMenu` now caches static model subtitles instead of querying runtime modes per row. Idle allocation root-cause profiling remains an Instruments/manual blocker. · ship-in-v1
- Landing agents / Companion Farm — patched · `LandingFarmView` is now a small top-right `AGENTS` dock with a compact `+` add button; `CompanionRoamingField` is a static landing-only shelf with one coarse 0.75s shared breathing clock, no roaming/walking, and no graph mounts; active agents are immediately usable because newly created agents activate by default and the active agent persona is injected into PipelineService and ChatCoordinator system prompts. Legacy orb rows decode but render as compact block agents in v1; creation now offers six block-style body presets, and the dock glyphs keep solid body silhouettes without the tiny internal belt/spine/mouth square dividers that read as visual noise at small size. Focused source guards pass, and the launched smoke confirmed the dock is visible/reachable and active agent selection changes AX state. · ship source patch; require longer idle CPU profiling before marking runtime Verified Fixed
- Landing click wave — patched · `LandingWaveDesign` now uses a denser 18x10-per-100pt character grid with a 16-character luminance ramp, while `LandingWaveChoreography` reduces impact/crown/crater/jet radii and slows the click sequence (`0.055s`, `0.105s`, `0.190s`, `0.310s`) so the puddle reads compact and deliberate rather than a large fast-radius bloom. Verified by the focused `LandingWaveChoreographyTests` and `LandingWaveGlyphAtlasTests` run above; live search opened successfully, but a one-shot CPU sample during active search was still elevated at 15.8%, so the animation path needs Time Profiler/tuning before RC. · keep source patch; do not mark performance Verified Fixed yet
- Agent spend dashboard — wired · `SpendDashboardHost` maps real `session_metrics` token/cache rows into `CostDashboardEntry` with `provider: nil` and `estimatedCostUSD: nil` until those columns exist, and `CostDashboardView` renders total cost as `—` plus row cost as `Not tracked` instead of synthetic `$0.00` spend. `SettingsCategoryTests.spendDashboardNeverReportsUntrackedCostsAsZero` source-guards the optional model, nil mapping, and absence of the old placeholder strings. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/SettingsCategoryTests` after an initial guard failure exposed and fixed a stale `$0.00 placeholder` comment; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-005732-53224.xcresult`. · ship token/cache transparency in v1; extend `session_metrics` before showing provider or per-session cost estimates
- Code editor search/find — wired · `CodeEditorSearchEngine` performs wrapped forward/backward, case-aware `NSString` range search; `CodeEditorView.performSearch` selects matches through `EpistemosEditorCoordinator.select(range:scrollToVisible:)`; `CodeEditorPolishTests` cover wrap, case sensitivity, `.notFound` cursor scaffold, and source removal of the previous `_ = direction` stub. · ship-in-v1
- Code editor large-file viewport policy — patched · `CodeEditorLargeFilePolicy` gates 100k-character/10k-line buffers into bounded visible-line windows; `SegmentedIndentationGuideView` can parse either an absolute viewport line range or a pre-sliced visible text window with an explicit base line number; `EpistemosEditorCoordinator` caches UTF-16 line-start offsets on text changes, refreshes indentation guides from cached text instead of re-reading `textView.string` on scroll, extracts visible text windows for large files, and tracks cursor selections against cached text instead of fetching `NSTextView.string` on movement; the dormant fallback gutter stays cold while hidden and `CodeLineGutterView` lazily caches visible labels instead of prebuilding one string per line. Focused `CodeEditorPolishTests` passed 29 tests with large-buffer policy/source guards. · keep native CodeEditSourceEditor path for v1; require launched-app Time Profiler/Animation Hitches on a large Swift/Rust file before promoting runtime issue to Verified Fixed
- Code editor semantic LSP — wired · `CodeEditorView` exposes on-demand Inspect Symbol and Go to Definition buttons only when both the file language and `agent_coreFFI` Rust runtime are available; unsupported/unlinked builds now disable the controls and show explicit unavailable help instead of an installed-looking action. `CodeEditorSemanticLSP` opens the live buffer through `RustLSPTransport` + `LSPClient`, calls real `textDocument/hover` / `textDocument/definition`, summarizes hover responses, selects same-file definition ranges through `EpistemosEditorCoordinator`, and reports cross-file definitions as found but not navigable. Verified by the 45-test LSP runtime-gate suite above. The unfinished semantic sidebar remains gated by `CodeEditorReleasePolicy.semanticSidebarEnabled = false`. · ship on-demand hover/same-file definition in v1 only for linked Swift/Rust runtime builds, defer full semantic sidebar/cross-file diagnostics UI
- LSP runtime substrate — wired · `RustLSPTransport` uses in-process FFI to the tree-sitter Rust/Swift `LspKernel`; the no-FFI Swift implementation fails closed, `InProcessLSPTransport` is labeled as a test-only lifecycle double, and visible editor bridge/source guards cover hover, definition, and runtime availability. · ship-in-v1
- Landing search composer — patched · `Epistemos/Views/Landing/LandingView.swift` uses a real visible native `TextField` for landing search, keeps the search wave non-hit-testing, keeps `/` slash commands, `@` note/chat references, cache indicator, and submit reachable, and uses a short `Ask Epistemos...` visible placeholder while preserving the longer `ComposerAttachmentEntryHints.landingPlaceholder` as accessibility guidance. `EpistemosTests/ComposerAttachmentEntryAuditTests.swift` source-guards the absence of the old fake paperclip affordance while preserving the shared attachment-entry teaching. Verified by the 281-test focused run and Computer Use live smoke above. · ship-in-v1; keep file attachment entry in the real main/mini composer paths until landing attachment is actually wired
- Companion adapter hot-swap — deferred · `CompanionRoamingField` no longer exposes the Landing Farm `Apply Adapter...` context-menu action, `LandingView` no longer mounts `CompanionAdapterView`, and the preserved scaffold renders `Adapter Pipeline Deferred` without path input, `Task.sleep`, or fake settled success. `CompanionAvatarGrammarSourceGuardTests.landingFarmDoesNotExposeDeferredAdapterHotSwapAsFakeApplyFlow` locks the gate. Verified by `./scripts/xcodebuild_epistemos.sh -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/CompanionAvatarGrammarSourceGuardTests`; passing result bundle `build/xcode-results/2026-05-08-010529-81565.xcresult`. · keep scaffold source-preserved; ship no adapter hot-swap UI until MLX loader, validation, and rollback are real
- Production crash-trap cleanup — wired · `LandingWaveRenderer` raw-buffer access, `EpdocDocument` default payload construction, and `LSPMessage` frame encoding avoid force unwraps; coder-only AppKit editor/graph scaffolds now return `nil` from failable `init(coder:)` instead of trapping; `NonAgentPruningValidationTests` source-guard these live files. · ship-in-v1
- iMessage routing — gated-correctly · The iMessage driver/settings surface is non-MAS only, starts disabled unless explicitly configured, and is hidden from the App Store build; the Pro-only Rust `imessage` tool reads Messages SQLite read-only, validates send inputs, bounds AppleScript output, and redacts/classifies send failures instead of exposing raw stderr. Verified by `cargo test --lib --features pro-build imessage`. Live Messages send/read permission smokes remain manual. · ship-in-v1
- HELIOS V5 runtime controls — deferred · `SettingsView.visibleSections` no longer lists `.heliosV5`; `HELIOSv5SettingsView` is a read-only deferred scaffold with no `@AppStorage("epistemos.helios.v5...")` toggles; `MessageBubble` no longer reads the VRM user default or renders a hardcoded `.plausibleButUnverified` chip before real AnswerPacket emission exists; `app-review-audit.sh` and focused Xcode source guards pass. · keep scaffold/tests/docs, but do not ship as a v1 feature.
````

## File 22: `docs/audits/V1_SHIP_GATE_DECISION.md`

- Top-level area: `docs`
- Lines: 77
- Bytes: 5,185
- Language fence: `markdown`

````markdown
# V1 Ship Gate Decision

Date: 2026-04-28

Decision: Mac App Store V1 should ship a small, native, sandbox-safe surface: Prose, Chat, Search, Graph, Settings/Privacy, local/cloud model management, and only the parts of Raw Thoughts, Contextual Shadows, Documents, and Code that are proven end to end. Powerful unfinished systems stay hidden or direct-build-only.

## Required Ship-Gate Table

| Feature | Current state | Ship V1? | Hide? | Remove? | Direct build only? | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Prose editor | Built/wired/core | YES | NO | NO | NO | Protected native thinking surface |
| Chat | Built/wired | YES | NO | NO | NO | Core workflow; provider routing/copy must stay exact |
| Search | Built with FTS/readable-block substrate | YES | NO | NO | NO | Must verify artifact/block jump targets |
| Graph | Built/wired | YES | NO | NO | NO | Keep current renderer; no risky graph rewrites before V1 |
| Settings/privacy | Built | YES | NO | NO | NO | MAS copy and tests required |
| Contextual Shadows | V0 state/UI exists behind env flag | YES if P1 proof passes | YES until proof | NO | NO | V1 differentiator; needs end-to-end and no-hitch proof |
| Instant Recall substrate | Built | YES | NO | NO | NO | Async-only rebuild required |
| Raw Thoughts | V0 Rust/Swift/UI exists behind env flag | YES if recovery/provenance tests pass | YES until proof | NO | NO | Must store observable surfaces only |
| `.epdoc` Documents | Built as package/NSDocument/Tiptap shell | MAYBE, gated | YES until smoke passes | NO | NO | Not absent; current gap is user-path proof |
| Code editor | Built with CodeEditSourceEditor and custom support | YES as current editor | NO | NO | NO | 4k-line performance/gutter proof required before marketing as high-performance |
| Code line gutter | Partial/existing logic | YES if benchmark/theme proof passes | YES if not proven | NO | NO | User requested refined non-conflicting line count |
| Quick Capture | Partial/unclear | TBD | YES if no proof | NO | NO | Must save to vault and update derived stores |
| Local model catalog/downloads | Built/partial | YES | NO | NO | NO | Storage and network disclosure required |
| Cloud providers | Built | YES | NO | NO | NO | User-sent content disclosure required |
| MCP safe built-ins | Built/partial | YES subset | NO | NO | NO | MAS only safe vault/search tools |
| Agent runtime | Built | LIMITED | Hide advanced toggles | NO | Direct build for risky tools | No reachable stubs or unsafe automation |
| Computer use | Built outside MAS, stubbed MAS | NO MAS | YES MAS | NO | YES | Screen/AX/CGEvent review risk |
| Shell/PTY/Docker tools | Built/partial | NO MAS | YES MAS | NO | YES | Arbitrary execution risk |
| iMessage automation | Built/partial | NO MAS until reviewed | YES | NO | YES likely | Sensitive entitlement/review risk |
| Agent Command Center | Partial | NO V1 unless already stable | YES | NO | MAYBE | Too broad for MAS V1 |
| Diagnostics panel | Partial | OPTIONAL hidden | YES by default | NO | NO | Useful for developer/test builds |
| Deterministic Knowledge Runtime v1 | Research/partial | NO V1 by default | YES | NO | NO | Needs separate preflight and end-to-end benchmarks |
| Tiptap advanced export DOCX/PDF | Not V1 | NO | YES | NO | NO | Exports are derived/on-demand later |

## P0 Must Clear Before V1

1. MAS build passes after all Settings/Info.plist/entitlement changes.
2. Computer-use, shell, PTY, Docker, and arbitrary external MCP execution are hidden or stubbed in MAS.
3. PrivacyInfo and Settings copy match actual data flow.
4. No reachable hard crash/stub in core open/edit/search/chat/graph/settings flows.
5. User canonical data is not overwritten by derived projections.

## P1 Must Clear Before Public Beta

1. Contextual Shadows V0 end-to-end test and no-hitch proof.
2. Raw Thoughts JSONL recovery and provider opaque preservation tests.
3. `.epdoc` package smoke if Documents are visible.
4. Code editor 4k-line performance and Unicode range tests.
5. Search/readable-block integration for every visible artifact kind.
6. Graph/search deletion and rename consistency proof.

## Hide / Keep Disabled For V1 Unless Proven

- Contextual Shadows if result routing/open behavior is not green.
- Raw Thoughts if JSONL recovery/provider-preservation tests are not green.
- Documents if open/save/search smoke is not green.
- Quick Capture if save/index/graph update is not green.
- Agent Command Center and broad automation.
- Diagnostics unless under Advanced/developer mode.

## Direct-Build-Only

- ScreenCaptureKit/AX/CGEvent computer use.
- Shell/PTY/Docker/local process tools.
- External MCP servers with arbitrary filesystem/process/network permissions.
- iMessage automation until App Store-specific review is complete.

## Final Ship Gate

V1 is approved only after:

- Pro and MAS builds pass from raw logs.
- Targeted P0/P1 tests pass.
- Manual-only Phase S gates are either completed later by the user or explicitly excluded from a non-ship development milestone.
- Bundle size and major resource sizes are recorded.
- Privacy/App Store review notes are current.
- No docs claim a hidden, flagged, or unproven feature is shipped.
````

````markdown
---
state: audit
created_on: 2026-05-06
scope: Epistemos V6.1 lean-canon implementation reality matrix
verdict: GREEN_FOR_THIS_SLICE_NOT_RELEASE_READY
---

# V6.1 Lean Reality Matrix - 2026-05-06

This ledger records what is actually proven in the current Epistemos
worktree after the V6.1 attention-as-interrupt audit pass. It exists to
prevent a subtle but dangerous drift: treating canonical target language
as if it were already hardware-proven implementation.

The north star is now pinned in code and doctrine:

> Epistemos is not an AI app. It is a cognitive substrate that
> occasionally summons AI as a precision instrument.
>
> The model is a guest in the user's brain - not a tenant of the user's
> machine.

The current slice is green for the surfaces below. It is not a final
release sign-off because runtime/manual app checks, distribution review,
sanitizer passes, and three no-change zero-fail release passes have not
been completed.

## Verified In Code And Tests

| Surface | Evidence | Result | Nuance |
|---|---|---:|---|
| AnswerPacket attention mode | `agent_core/src/scope_rex/answer_packet.rs`; `Epistemos/Models/AnswerPacket.swift`; W1 falsifier | PASS | `attention_mode` is present in Rust and Swift as `dynamic`, `static_fallback`, or `unavailable`. Default is `unavailable`. |
| Static 9:1 fallback honesty | `ClaimKind::StaticFallbackAcknowledged`; AnswerPacket helpers; doctrine linter gate 6.1 | PASS | Static fallback must be acknowledged. A fallback emission without the acknowledgement claim is invalid by helper and source-lint contract. |
| Goodfire VPD precision | `epistemos-research/src/goodfire_vpd_specs.rs`; canonical consistency tests | PASS | Internal math is `205 / 9972`, while user-facing/canonical presentation is `2.1%`. Comments and labels must not fight the rounded canon. |
| V6.1 five-plane discipline | `epistemos-research/src/v6_1_stream_surface.rs`; `v6_1_execution_policy.rs`; canonical consistency tests | PASS | Streams are product lanes. Planes are runtime structure. The tests preserve all 15 stream-plane cells. |
| ACS plane placement | `epistemos-research/src/acs.rs` | PASS | ACS lives in the Episodic plane and is audited in the Verification plane. |
| MAS / Pro / Vault execution policy | `epistemos-research/src/v6_1_execution_policy.rs` | PASS | MAS uses interrupt-first policy with static 9:1 fallback only when dynamic signals are unavailable. Pro enables full interrupt scoring plus LocalRecallIsland policy. Vault adds PacketRouter1bit and experimental ConnectomeAlarm policy. |
| Kernel canon posture | `epistemos-research/src/m2_max_kernels.rs` | PASS | The V6.1 five kernels are recorded as doctrine targets, not implementation claims. |
| LocalAgent membrane | `Epistemos/LocalAgent/LocalAgentPromptBuilder.swift`; `agent_core/src/agent_runtime/prompt_format.rs` | PASS | External/provider/tool orchestration is a membrane. Local deterministic substrate answers must not add a gateway hop when no external context is needed. |
| Source mirror coverage | `Epistemos.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`; HELIOS source guards | PASS | The app test bundle now mirrors doctrine, research, vault, shadow, and Lean source files, including `.lean` and `lean-toolchain`. |
| Lean theorem budget | `Tools/sorry-budget/sorry-budget.sh --report` | PASS | Current budget is 37 total sorries. This is not "100% theorem proven"; it is an explicit, bounded Lean proof debt ledger. |
| SSD/RAM mmap substrate | `agent_core/src/arena/mod.rs`; `agent_core/tests/arena_budget.rs` | PASS | A real temp-file-backed `MAP_SHARED` arena is opened twice. Producer writes and flushes, consumer reads and consumes, producer observes tail update, and a reopened mapping preserves header state. |
| Current real Metal shaders | `Tools/metal-shader-compile/metal-shader-compile.sh` | PASS | 19 existing `.metal` files compile with `xcrun -sdk macosx metal -std=metal3.1`. |
| W25 theorem/falsifier runner | `Tools/falsifier/falsifier.sh` | PASS | All registered W25 protocols pass. This is a stage-0 protocol pass, not a substitute for every V6.1 hardware falsifier. |
| Swift warning cleanup | `EpistemosSpeechAnalyzer.swift`; `AppBootstrap.swift`; Epdoc, MLX thermal, and agent harness tests | PASS | The prior warning inventory was retired. The full post-cleanup macOS suite passed after stabilizing a notification timing assertion. |

## Verification Commands Run

| Command | Result |
|---|---:|
| `cargo test --manifest-path agent_core/Cargo.toml` | PASS: 985 lib tests plus binary, integration, and doc-test suites. |
| `cargo test --manifest-path epistemos-research/Cargo.toml --features research` | PASS: 464 lib tests, 97 canonical consistency tests, doc tests. |
| `Tools/metal-shader-compile/metal-shader-compile.sh` | PASS: 19 Metal shaders compile. |
| `Tools/sorry-budget/sorry-budget.sh --report` | PASS: 37 total sorries. |
| `Tools/falsifier/falsifier.sh` | PASS: W25 all protocols. |
| `xcodebuild -quiet -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' build` | PASS. |
| `xcodebuild -quiet -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test -only-testing:EpistemosTests/SearchFusionHealthRowTests` | PASS: focused regression check after notification-counter stabilization. |
| `xcodebuild -quiet -project Epistemos.xcodeproj -scheme Epistemos -destination 'platform=macOS' test` | PASS: 254.086 second post-cleanup full test run. |
| `cd graph-engine && cargo test` | PASS: 2522 passed, 8 ignored, doc tests. |
| `cd omega-mcp && cargo test` | PASS: 143 passed, doc test ignored. |
| `cd omega-ax && cargo test` | PASS: 12 passed. |
| `cd epistemos-vault && cargo test --features vault` | PASS: 15 passed, doc tests. |
| `git diff --check` | PASS. |

## Current Warning Inventory

The prior Swift warning inventory has been retired in this slice:

- `EpistemosSpeechAnalyzer.swift`: converter state is isolated behind a small locked `@unchecked Sendable` helper.
- `AppBootstrap.swift`: the redundant `await` was removed.
- `EpdocEndToEndSmokeTests.swift`: unnecessary `try` expressions were removed.
- `MLXThermalBenchTests.swift`: intentionally discarded benchmark results are now explicit.
- `AgentHarnessTests.swift`: deprecated string loading was replaced with the encoding-aware initializer.

The only observed Xcode chatter in the latest quiet build/test output is
the standard "multiple matching destinations" destination selection
warning and run-script phase notes. Keep watching non-quiet logs during
the release audit; this is not a substitute for manual/runtime log review.

## Proxy Passes Versus Hardware Proof

| Claim area | Current status | Why it is not enough yet |
|---|---|---|
| V6.1 `SemiseparableBlockScan.metal` | Target only | Existing Mamba2 shaders compile, but no canonical `SemiseparableBlockScan.metal` file is implemented and bit-exact checked against `cartesia-metal`. |
| `LocalRecallIsland.metal` | Target only | Policy is encoded. The actual passkey recall >= 0.99 at 128K is not yet hardware-run. |
| `PageGather.metal` | Target only | No M2 Max gather benchmark proves >= 70% memory bandwidth utilization yet. |
| `ControllerKernelPack.metal` | Target only | No fused controller pack exists with 1 ULP equivalence proof yet. |
| `PacketRouter1bit.metal` | Target only | Vault policy is encoded. No implemented canonical kernel has proven routing-quality loss <= 2% versus FP16 reference. |
| `InterruptScore.metal` | Target only | The interrupt equation exists in research code. The always-on Metal kernel is not implemented yet. |
| T35 at 128K | Not hardware-proven | `rho_max = 0.20` is encoded. Real RULER/passkey long-context runs are still required. |
| T42 ConnectomeAlarm | Research/Vault target | Goodfire atlas status is public-confirmed for observability, but runtime prediction of interrupt traces remains unproven. |
| Donor-distilled student | Not trained | The Qwen3-8B to Granite-4-H-shape route is canonical. No student checkpoint exists in this repo yet. |

## Next Gates Before New-Repo V1

1. Add real hardware falsifier harnesses for the V6.1 target kernels before claiming kernel completion.
2. Implement `InterruptScore.metal` only after the CPU/reference interrupt-score contract is wired into AnswerPacket emission.
3. Add a runtime/manual app audit with logs for model routing, fallback behavior, note AI, Omega/research, and settings copy.
4. Run sanitizer passes when feasible.
5. Run the release-audit recursive zero-fail rule only after code changes pause.

## Bottom Line

The current Epistemos build is materially more canonical and lean after
this pass: the audit substrate now records whether attention was dynamic,
static fallback, or unavailable; static fallback is no longer silent;
Goodfire precision no longer fights the 2.1% canon; ACS and the tier/plane
rules are executable tests; mmap has a real SSD-backed producer/consumer
proof; and the existing Metal shaders compile.

The V6.1 five-kernel substrate is still a target, not a finished hardware
claim. That honesty is part of the architecture. The floor never moves,
and the proof ledger should never pretend it did.

## V6.2 Intake Link - 2026-05-07

The follow-on V6.2 research has been saved and indexed at
`docs/fusion/EPISTEMOS_V6_2_CANON_INTAKE_2026_05_07.md`.

The important update is not a product rename. It is a verification
envelope correction: Jojo's M2 Pro 16GB is now the shippability rig, and
M2 Max / workstation-class targets are scale-validation or Pro/Vault
only. V6.2 preserves the V6.1 architecture while budget-revising the
hardware falsifiers:

- PageGather must pass against 70% of measured `BW_baseline_M2Pro`, not
  against theoretical M2 Max bandwidth.
- LocalRecallIsland Core is 32K, 50 trials x 5 depths; 128K is Stretch.
- SemiseparableBlockScan Core is L=32768; L=131072 is Stretch.
- InterruptScore is Swift CPU canonical for the single-token path; Metal
  remains a batch shadow only.
- Goodfire VPD activity subnumbers were revalidated by Codex on the live
  public page during intake, while runtime acceleration remains candidate.
````
